3. Photographically produced plans of, and copies of plans of, land to be sufficient
compliance with law requiring plans or copies of plans to be drawn

5. Power of Minister to amend Schedule
SCHEDULE
5
LAWS OF MALAYSIA
Act 233
LAND AND MINING PLANS AND DOCUMENTS
(PHOTOGRAPHIC COPIES) ACT 1950
An Act to provide for the validity of photographically produced plans
and copies of plans and documents.
[23 May 1950]
Short title and application

1.  (1)  This Act may be cited as the Land and Mining Plans and
Documents (Photographic Copies) Act 1950.
(2)  This Act shall apply only in *Peninsular Malaysia.
Interpretation
2.
In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires—
“document” means—
(a) a register document of title, an issue document of title or a
lease, as defined in each case in the National Land Code
[Act No. 56 of 1965];
(b) any licence, permit, notice, agreement or other document
relating to land granted, issued or entered into by or on
behalf of the Ruler or Yang di-Pertua Negeri of a State; or
____________________________________________________________________________________
*NOTE—Application to Federal Territory of Labuan with modification −see P.U. (A) 457/2009 with
effect from 1 January 2010.
6                                       Laws of Malaysia                           ACT 233
(c) a mining lease or sub-lease, a provisional mining lease or a
mining certificate granted or issued under any written law
specified in the Schedule;
“Peninsular Malaysia” has the meaning assigned thereto by
section 3 of the Interpretation Acts 1948 and 1967 [Act 388], and
includes the Federal Territory.
Photographically produced plans of, and copies of plans of, land
to be sufficient compliance with law requiring plans or copies of
plans to be drawn
3.
Where, under the provisions of any written law specified in the
Schedule, a plan or copy of a plan of any land is required to be drawn
on, contained in or attached to any document, or any copy of any
document is required to be made or submitted to any person, it shall
be sufficient for the purposes of any such written law to attach to
such document a photographically produced plan or copy of a plan of
such land or to make or submit a photographically produced copy of
such document, as the case may be:
Provided that—
(a) such photographically produced plan or copy of any plan
or document is made by the Director General of Survey or
by a person duly authorized in writing by him so to do;
and
(b) such plan or copy is certified by the Director General of
Survey or the person making the same to be a true and
correct plan of such land or to be a true and accurate copy
of the original plan or of the original document, as the
case may be.
Land and Mining Plans and Documents
7
(Photographic Copies)
Photographic plan and copies to be admissible in evidence
4.
Every photographically produced plan of any land or copy of a
plan or of a document duly made and certified in accordance with
section 3 and attached to any document or submitted to any person as
required by, and for the purpose of, any written law relating to land or
mining in Peninsular Malaysia shall be admissible in evidence
without further proof in all courts in Peninsular Malaysia.
Power of Minister to amend Schedule
5.
The Minister charged with responsibility for lands may from
time to time by order published in the Gazette amend or add to the
Schedule.
______________________
